name: nvim-buffer-sync
description: Synchronize opened tabs and buffers between Neovim (running in integrated terminal) and VS Code

commands.title: Neovim Buffer Sync: Connect to Neovim
Neovim Buffer Sync: Disconnect from Neovim
Neovim Buffer Sync: Show Connection Status

nvimBufferSync.socketPath.description: Path to Neovim's listen socket. If empty, auto-discovers common socket paths (fixed /tmp names, NVIM_LISTEN_ADDRESS, /tmp/nvim…/0).

nvimBufferSync.autoConnect.description: Retry connecting on an interval and when a new integrated terminal opens (socket discovery; does not inspect terminal contents).

nvimBufferSync.syncVscodeToNvim.description: When a file is opened in VS Code, also open it in Neovim's buffer list.

nvimBufferSync.syncNvimToVscode.description: When a buffer is opened in Neovim, also open it in VS Code.

nvimBufferSync.openBehavior.description: How to open files in VS Code when synced from Neovim. 'background' opens a persistent (non-preview) tab and keeps focus in the terminal. 'preview' uses a single preview tab that the next open can replace. 'active' opens a persistent tab and switches focus to the editor.

nvimBufferSync.ignoredPatterns.description: Substring patterns; paths containing any entry are skipped for sync in both directions.

nvimBufferSync.syncDiffAndReviewTabs.description: When a diff/review tab is open in VS Code, add the underlying file: paths to Neovim (TabInputTextDiff modified/original; TabInputCustom with a file URI).

nvimBufferSync.closePreviewBuffersInNvim.description: When openBehavior is 'preview' and Nvim→VS Code replaces the preview tab, remove the previous file's Neovim buffer if safe. (Closing the last VS Code tab for a file opened from VS Code also attempts safe removal regardless of this flag.)

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run check && npm run compile
